Refactor DSP to stereo, fix drum preset export, and misc improvements

- Waveform display and zero-crossing use L channel

commit.message:
Fix false positives in exploit/scan modules (FW3.7.1)

- Add _discover_params(): crawl real page params before testing
- SQLi: use baseline comparison to filter pre-existing SQL-like text
- SQLi: time-based threshold now requires +4s vs baseline (not raw 2.5s)
- XSS: use unique session marker instead of generic payloads
- XSS: remove DOM sink false positives (innerHTML/eval exist everywhere)
- CMDi: use echo CMDI_CONFIRMED marker, remove generic 'ttl' indicator
- LFI: remove empty indicator payload (matched everything)
- LFI: use specific indicators (root:x:0:0:, HTTP_HOST=)
- vBulletin: require 'vbulletin' in response body (not just 302)
- upload_check: only report 200 with upload form content (not 403)
- sqli_scan: discover real params from page before injecting

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Release WebGate FW3.7

New features:
- SSH backdoor via RCE CVE with Scope Agreement (typed 'I accept')
- TOR routing via bridge (obfs4/webtunnel/snowflake)
- SQLMap interactive dump dialog: pick DB -> table -> dump in GUI
- CVEExploitOfferDialog: auto-prompt when RCE CVE detected after scan
- ScopeAgreementDialog: typed 'I accept' confirmation before exploit
- SSHBackdoorDialog: configure SSH credentials + TOR bridge
- SQLMapDumpDialog: GUI-based DB/table selector with background dump

Improvements:
- Simplified reports: SQL injection Y/N + vulnerable param/payload
- XSStrike output simplified to injection Y/N + vulnerable URL
- Progress bar with gradient, % overlay, bright pulse edge animation
- Logo glitch animation (random interval glitch effect)
- Particle burst animation on scan start
- FW badge in logo canvas
- Scan-line border animation during active scan

Fixes:
- Removed COPY button (caused GUI freeze/lag on Wayland)
- Version strings updated to FW3.7
- LICENSE added (authorized use only)
- README.md fully rewritten with all features documented

Add defensive handling to all Founder Console pages

Fix runtime errors by adding comprehensive defensive programming with optional chaining and safe defaults throughout all founder pages:

Platform Dashboard (founder/page.tsx):
- Add optional chaining for all data.growth properties (organizationsLast7d, organizationsLast30d, etc.)
- Add safe defaults for metrics (data?.organizations ?? 0)
- Add safe defaults for arrays (data?.trends?.analysisRunsByDay ?? [])
- Add safe defaults for alerts arrays before .map() calls
- Add optional chaining for exposureDistribution properties

Billing Dashboard (founder/billing/page.tsx):
- Add optional chaining for metrics.revenue properties (mrr, arr, avgRevenuePerOrg)
- Add optional chaining for metrics.subscriptions properties (total, byStatus, byPlan)
- Add optional chaining for metrics.seats properties (total, avgPerOrg)
- Add safe defaults for alerts arrays before .map() and .length calls
- Fix Object.entries() calls with safe defaults

Organizations List (founder/organizations/page.tsx):
- Add safe default for organizations array before .filter() calls (organizations ?? [])

Organization Detail (founder/organizations/[orgId]/page.tsx):
- Fix filteredUsers to use safe default (org?.users ?? []) before chaining
- Fix filteredStudies to use safe default (org?.studies ?? []) before chaining

This prevents "Cannot read properties of undefined" errors when API responses are incomplete or undefined.

commit.message:
Wire Buscar Casas to direct scrape endpoint, bypass LLM agent

The BuscadorAgent delegated scraping to the LLM which was unreliable:
it would report "success" without actually saving listings. The
existing POST /api/market-listings/scrape endpoint already does
everything correctly (scrape, 60% rule, zone check, price range,
save qualified, persist images).

- Buscar Casas now calls /api/market-listings/scrape for 4 cities
  (Houston, Dallas, San Antonio, Austin) sequentially
- Added Next.js proxy route for /api/market-listings/scrape
- Toast shows real counts (scraped, qualified, saved)
- BuscadorAgent process() also made deterministic (no LLM orchestration)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: CNI plugins sha256sum verification — .sha256 file already contains filename

The GitHub release .sha256 file format is `<hash>  <filename>`, so the
previous `echo "$(cat sums)  $TGZ"` doubled the filename, causing
sha256sum to fail. Use `sha256sum -c "$CNI_SUMS"` directly.

This caused the install-nomad.sh script to fail on first boot, preventing
CNI plugins installation, volume directory creation, and Docker/Nomad
service startup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Security remediation + comprehensive UX/accessibility audit fixes across 29 files

Security fixes (from prior audit - 16 findings):
- Path traversal prevention in EncryptedBackupService
- Reference number overflow guard
- Thread-safe token caching in GoogleDriveService
- Rate limit handling and auth retry in SubmissionViewModel
- Declaration validation expansion with conditional field checks
- File size alerts for document uploads
- NRIC masking for public kiosk privacy
- Force-unwrap elimination and sanitization hardening

UX/Performance fixes (27 findings):
- P3: Force-dismiss keyboard before validation to flush FormField buffers
- P1/P5: Remove dead syncTask code, add shake animation guard
- P2/V5: Gate animations behind accessibilityReduceMotion
- P6: Reuse NWPathMonitor dispatch queue
- U1: Reduce welcome entrance animation from 1.2s to 0.4s
- U3: Add visible countdown text to confirmation screen
- U4: Change "Registration" to "Job Application" for clarity
- U7: Add "Copy Reference" button on confirmation
- U8: Standardize date pickers to .compact style
- U10: Scroll to top on validation failure via ScrollViewReader
- U11: Show submit guidance when required fields incomplete
- U12: Add "Sign here" watermark to signature canvas
- U16: Add "Try Again" button to submission error alert
- U2: Cycling submission overlay messages for progress feedback
- V1: Darken mediumGray #6B7280 -> #4B5563 for WCAG AA contrast
- V2: Increase welcome subtext opacity 0.4 -> 0.7
- V3: Increase section label size 11pt -> 13pt
- V6: Add standardized animation tokens (.snappy, .smooth, .dramatic)
- A3: Merge ConsentBlock accessibility elements for VoiceOver
- R1/R2/R3: Responsive glow scaling, safe area bottom padding
- U5: Clarify "Years Known (approx.)" label
- U6: Extend idle timeout to 12min warn / 15min reset (3-min window)
- U9: Add autocapitalization parameter to FormField

Build: SUCCEEDED | Tests: 107 run, 0 regressions (6 pre-existing)

fix: GitLab OIDC — root CA trust, real client secret, PKCE, admin promotion

- Root CA secret: split into separate .crt files (one cert per file) so
  update-ca-certificates picks them up; .pem extension was silently skipped
- OIDC client secret: read from kv/oidc/gitlab (Keycloak source) instead
  of using stale placeholder from kv/services/gitlab
- PKCE: disable S256 for GitLab client in Keycloak — GitLab omniauth
  doesn't send code_challenge params (confidential client, low risk)
- Admin promotion: auto-promote GITLAB_ADMIN_OIDC_USER via rails runner
  after deploy (idempotent, skips if user hasn't logged in yet)

commit.message:
fix(security): remove raw encryption key from team invite URLs

Team AES-256-GCM key was embedded as ?key= in invite URLs, exposing it
in clipboard history, chat logs, etc. Now uses passphrase-based key
wrapping (PBKDF2 600k + AES-GCM) with server-side one-time claim tokens.
Admin shares passphrase separately via secure channel.

- team-crypto.ts: add wrapKeyWithPassphrase/unwrapKeyWithPassphrase
- use-team.ts: new invite flow with claim tokens, legacy backwards compat
- team-section.tsx: passphrase display for admin, passphrase input for member
- 28 tests pass including new passphrase wrapping tests

commit.message:
Add raymarching cost heatmap render mode

New "COST" mode (cycle with m key) shows per-pixel raymarching
step count as a heatmap:
- Blue = few steps (fast/cheap)
- Green = moderate
- Yellow/Red = many steps (slow/expensive)
- Darkened background for missed rays

Reveals performance bottlenecks: thin geometry, complex booleans,
and areas where the SDF is slow to converge.

Uses raymarchCost() which returns vec2(distance, stepCount).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

add marketplace / monetization rail messaging for tapers and fans

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: DST timezone bug causing duplicate March 8 and missing April 1

generateAllDays() mixed UTC (toISOString) with local time (getDate/setDate),
which caused March 8 to appear twice when DST springs forward, dropped
April 1 from the program, and shifted all day-of-week values by one.

Fix: use local Date constructor and getLocalDateStr() consistently.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: eliminate clicks at block boundaries (OscillatorBank amplitude interpolation bug)

Root cause: setTargetAmplitudes computed deltas from stale m_amplitudes
(the START of the previous ramp) instead of the interpolated end value.
Each block's amplitude snapped back to the old start before ramping to
the new target, causing audible discontinuities every 1024 samples.

Fix: finalize in-progress interpolation before computing new deltas.

Additional anti-click hardening:
- Output gain: per-sample linear ramp instead of per-buffer step
- Silence crossfade: fade-in when source transitions from silent to active
- Retrigger fade: doubled from 64 to 128 samples (~2.7ms at 48kHz)
- Envelope: minimum 1ms release prevents hard snap to zero

commit.message:
fix(api): correct proxy backend health endpoints (CAB-1725) (#1530)

## Summary
- **Linear**: `/graphql` → `/` (GraphQL needs POST with body, GET
returns 400)
- **Cloudflare**: `/user/tokens/verify` → `/` (requires auth token, GET
without token returns 400)
- **Pushgateway**: `/-/healthy` → `NULL` (unreachable from K8s cluster)

DB already patched in prod via direct UPDATE. This updates the migration
seed data for consistency on future deployments.

## Test plan
- [x] Verified all 8 backends healthy from API pod
- [x] n8n ✅, linear ✅, github ✅, slack-bot ✅, infisical ✅, cloudflare ✅
- [x] slack-webhook + pushgateway: no health endpoint (expected)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Dispatch circuit breaker: stop retrying after N consecutive failures (Forge-30y) (#58)

* feat: dispatch circuit breaker stops retrying after N consecutive failures

Track consecutive dispatch failures per bead in a new dispatch_failures
column on the retries table. After 3 consecutive failures (claim fails,
pipeline errors, smith producing no diff), the bead is marked needs_human=1
with a circuit-breaker reason, preventing it from consuming capacity every
poll cycle.

Changes:
- Add dispatch_failures column to retries table (auto-migrated)
- Add IncrementDispatchFailures, ResetDispatchFailures, and
  DispatchCircuitBrokenBeadIDSet to state.DB
- Record dispatch failures in claimBead and dispatchBead error paths
- Skip circuit-broken (needs_human=1) beads in pollAndDispatch
- Clear dispatch failures on successful pipeline completion
- Add "retry_bead" IPC command to reset the circuit breaker
- Add "forge queue retry" CLI command for human-initiated reset
- Manual dispatch via "forge queue run" auto-resets the breaker
- Refactor retry row scanning into shared scanRetryRows helper
- Add EventDispatchCircuitBreak event type for observability

Bead: Forge-30y

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-30y

- daemon.go: only reset dispatch circuit breaker in run_bead handler when
  dispatch_failures > 0, preventing unrelated needs_human state from being cleared
- db.go IncrementDispatchFailures: wrap multi-step upsert in a transaction for
  true atomicity and consistent count reads under concurrency
- db.go ResetDispatchFailures: scope UPDATE to rows with dispatch_failures > 0
  AND last_error LIKE 'circuit breaker:%' so non-circuit-breaker needs_human
  states are preserved
- db.go DispatchCircuitBrokenBeadIDSet: narrow query to last_error LIKE
  'circuit breaker:%' to exclude unrelated needs_human beads (e.g. exhausted retries)
- daemon_test.go: add retry_bead IPC tests covering invalid payload, missing
  fields, happy-path circuit breaker reset, and non-circuit-breaker preservation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Robin Smith <Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Optimize dataset format: Convert MAT to NPZ and update .gitignore

- Added convert_mat_to_npz.py script to convert MATLAB format files to NumPy compressed format
- Successfully converted all 13 dataset files from .mat to .npz format
  * Reduced dataset footprint from 130 MB to 120.5 MB (7.3% reduction)
  * All original data integrity preserved
  * Both acc, adl, fall, and two_classes datasets converted
- Updated .gitignore to ignore large data files (*.mat and *.npz)
  * Only source code and lightweight configuration files are tracked
  * Users must regenerate .npz files locally using the conversion script
  * Maintains repository size under 100 MB
- Added load_example_npz.py in src/ with example code for loading NPZ files with TensorFlow/Keras
- Removed original MAT files to save local disk space (130 MB freed)

Data can be reconstructed by:
1. Obtaining the UniMiB-SHAR dataset
2. Running: python convert_mat_to_npz.py
